nodes.name: Methods
nodes.description: Introduces CSPG (Cross-Partition Sparse Proximity Graph) method with two key algorithms: partitioning algorithm that randomly divides dataset into groups with routing vectors, and search algorithm using two-stage approach combining fast proximity search with cross-partition precise expansion to balance speed and accuracy.

nodes.name: Conclusion
nodes.description: Summarizes that CSPG achieves higher QPS and Recall@10 than traditional proximity graph implementations by creating smaller, sparser graphs per partition and using efficient two-stage search with routing vectors for cross-partition expansion.

edges.reason: Methods section (0) logically leads to Conclusion (1) as the methods implementation and experimental results are summarized in the conclusion
Conclusion (1) references back to the methods (0) by summarizing the novel CSPG schema and experimental results from the methods section
